title: Careers at Environment and Climate Change Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Careers at Environment and Climate Change Canada
You can be at the heart of domestic and international efforts to protect the environment, to lead action on climate change, to conserve our country’s natural heritage, and to provide weather and meteorological information to keep Canadians informed and safe.
ECCC employs over 7,600 people working in a broad range of fields including biology, chemistry, climatology, communications, engineering, environmental sciences, human resources, hydrology, informatics, law, law enforcement, library science, meteorology, policy, and more.  Our offices are found in over 100 communities across the country, from Iqaluit to Toronto and from Vancouver to St. John’s. We are passionate about promoting the value of science and technology to decision-making, collaborating with one another and with external stakeholders, both within our communities and around the world, and seeking the most innovative and effective solutions to protect our air, water, wildlife and spaces.
- We tackle some of the biggest environmental issues
- We embrace and promote diversity and inclusiveness
- We promote a respectful workplace
- We support a healthy work-life balance
- We invest in employee learning and career development
- We offer competitive wages and benefits
Protecting the environment starts with you. Come join us!
Current openings at ECCC Check out the positions we are staffing right now.
Opportunities for students in the federal government Discover employment programs for secondary, college and university students.
